text:
8: Part 3 (8—③, Hachi (San)) is the 237 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Killua is struck in the leg by a third flea bullet, quickly pulling it out and noting that the first wound in his temple won't stop bleeding. Flutter gives the shooter updated coordinates, and he steadies his breathing as the back of his gun swells to a large size. He fires a fourth shot and Killua is struck in the arm, realizing that the shooter's goal is to wear him down from blood loss. The shooter continues to hum a tune, proud of the fact that his air gun gives his fleas a vertical jump of 200 meters. He fires a fifth shot and Killua barely dodges as the bullet grazes his cheek, impressing Flutter. Killua knows that the shooter won't be able to aim at him if he picks up speed, but he doesn't want to waste any energy. Killua's aura swells around him, and Flutter is curious to see if he reveals his ability. The shooter fires another shot, and Flutter says that with his ability, Super Eye, he is able to see his target from any angle through his "Satellite Dragonflies."

It's explained that Killua is unable to use En since he isn't skilled enough to do so. But he extends his aura away from his body as much as he can (57 centimeters). With the previous shot, he waited until the bullet entered his aura, and then reacted with superhuman reflexes to dodge it. This time, however, he doesn't dodge the bullet and lets it strike his right temple, allowing him to calculate the bullet's trajectory so he can go after the shooter. Killua sprints through the jungle and leaps through the tree branches, with the shooter calmly standing up and ready to confront him. Killua notices that the small wound on his cheek stopped bleeding, and guesses that it's due to the flea's saliva since the bullet only grazed him. He says he can't get hit anymore and resolves to not let the shooter getaway.

Finally out of the jungle, Killua spots the shooter on his high perch, and he quickly jumps down and flees. Killua tracks him to the entrance of a cave, and the shooter sings that it's a trap, asking if he'll still follow as he leaps down. Instantly and with no hesitation, Killua appears directly behind him and uses his middle finger to pierce his brain. Killua tells him to die, but the shooter's eyes roll back in his head and tentacles suddenly emerge from his head and mouth, wrapping up Killua as the two fall toward an underground lake. The shooter asks him if he wants to see hell, and several creatures are seen approaching from below.
